foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Understanding the intrahepatic and extrahepatic biliary tree anatomy is essential to localize the level and cause of obstruction

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Cholecystitis workup often reveals concomitant biliary obstruction; understanding gallbladder inflammation contextualizes the jaundice evaluation

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: CBD stones are the most common cause of surgical obstructive jaundice and the primary differential in acute presentations

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Biliary obstruction complicated by infection leads to cholangitis — a surgical emergency requiring urgent biliary decompression

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Periampullary and gallbladder malignancies present as painless obstructive jaundice with Courvoisier's sign

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Post-surgical and inflammatory biliary strictures are important benign causes of obstructive jaundice requiring differentiation from malignancy

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Congenital choledochal cysts present with the classic triad of jaundice, pain, and abdominal mass, especially in children
